levels.label: Tourist
levels.copy: The Founder-Market Fit pattern is still mostly informal or ad hoc. Pick one stabilising action, document it, and avoid adding a long process before the basics are real.

levels.label: Researcher
levels.copy: Some Founder-Market Fit practices exist, but they still depend on individual memory and effort. The next step is two repeatable routines with clear owners.

levels.label: Adjacent Insider
levels.copy: Core Founder-Market Fit practices are repeatable, but evidence quality and ownership are uneven. Work on the weakest dimension before optimising the strongest one.

levels.label: Embedded Founder
levels.copy: Founder-Market Fit practices are measured and connected to decisions. The main work is now removing the constraint that prevents the system from scaling cleanly.

levels.label: Unfairly Positioned
levels.copy: Founder-Market Fit is operating as a learning system. Keep calibrating against evidence, stress-test resilience, and avoid treating a strong score as a permanent guarantee.

recommendations.domain-edge: Write a one-page non-obvious market truths memo and test each claim with domain experts.
recommendations.customer-access: Build a repeatable recruitment channel that can deliver 10 qualified conversations per month.
recommendations.credibility-and-assets: Acquire one high-trust proof point: paid pilot, reference, proprietary dataset, or recognised partner.
recommendations.long-term-pull-and-learning: Create a six-month domain-learning plan with explicit unknowns, experts, and falsifiable beliefs.

defaultSecondAction: Retake the diagnostic after a focused 30-day improvement cycle and compare only your own progress unless a team comparison is explicitly agreed.
reflectionQuestion: Which assumption behind your weakest dimension deserves the fastest evidence check?
methodologyNote: The current version uses four dimensions with three questions each. Dimension scores are normalised to 0-100. The balanced index weights 70% average dimension performance and 30% the weakest dimension. No percentile or benchmark is shown until a compatible sample exists.
shareNote: Share cards reveal only the level and strongest area. They do not publish individual answers, sensitive weaknesses, legal or financial details, security gaps, or family-specific information.
